text:
The AI-as-premium-SKU playbook is breaking, and most PMs haven't noticed yet.

The math is simple. Classic SaaS gross margins sit around 80-90%. AI features run closer to 50-60% once you account for inference costs. So when you bolt an AI add-on onto your product and charge 30% more for it, you're often selling a worse-margin product to a customer who's already paying ChatGPT $20 a month for the same output.

I've watched two patterns work. The rest are margin traps.

The ones that work either bury the AI so deep in the workflow that ripping it out would break the job (bundled pricing, no separate SKU), or they tie payment to a measurable outcome the agent delivers. Everything in between, the premium toggle, the AI tier, the credits pack bolted onto a seat license, is a holding pattern.

The question I'd push every PM on right now: if your AI feature disappeared tomorrow, would your customers churn, or would they shrug and open another tab?

If it's the second one, you don't have a pricing problem. You have a positioning problem dressed up as a pricing problem.

#AIProduct #SaaS #ProductManagement #B2BSaaS #AIStrategy #ProductStrategy
topic: AI features in B2B SaaS products

diagram.title: THE AI MONETIZATION LADDER
diagram.subtitle: How AI features earn their price, from margin trap to defensible moat

steps.label: PREMIUM TOGGLE
steps.description: Bolt-on SKU at 30-100% uplift. Easy to cancel, easy to copy.

steps.label: CREDITS & METERING
steps.description: Usage-based pricing that exposes your COGS to the customer.

steps.label: BUNDLED WORKFLOW
steps.description: AI woven into the core job. Removing it breaks the product.

steps.label: OUTCOME-BASED
steps.description: You get paid when the agent delivers a measurable result.

steps.label: ACCOUNTABLE AGENT
steps.description: Pricing tied to SLAs on quality, accuracy, or revenue impact.
